highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.169 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds a `--safe-mode` flag (or `CLAUDE_CODE_SAFE_MODE` env variable) that launches Claude Code with all customizations disabled — handy for troubleshooting when hooks, MCP servers, or plugins behave unexpectedly. It also introduces the `/cd` command so you can switch your working directory mid-session without losing your prompt cache, saving tokens and keeping context intact.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.166: Fallback Models and Glob Deny Rules
highlights.summary: You can now configure up to three fallback models in the `fallbackModel` setting, so Claude Code automatically switches to a backup model if your primary one is overloaded or unavailable — great for uninterrupted workflows. The update also adds glob pattern support in deny rules, letting you block entire categories of tools with a single `"*"` wildcard instead of listing each one individually.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.8
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.8, the latest upgrade to their most capable model tier. Developers using Claude Code can expect improvements in reasoning and code quality, particularly for complex multi-step tasks handled by the main agent.

highlights.title: Show HN: Rayline Routes Claude Code Subagents to Cheaper and On-Device Models
highlights.summary: Rayline is an LLM gateway compatible with Claude Code that intercepts internal model routing, letting you run the main agent on Opus while delegating subagent calls to cheaper or on-device models. This can significantly cut API costs during large agentic tasks without sacrificing quality on the steps that matter most.

highlights.title: Show HN: Lessons Learned from Running Claude Code Swarms at Scale
highlights.summary: A developer shares hard-won insights from orchestrating large fleets of Claude Code agents using a Python-based "fleet" tool with centralized coordination. The post covers practical gotchas around concurrency, error recovery, and prompt design when running many agents in parallel — essential reading before you scale up.

highlights.title: Headroom: Menu Bar App That Doubles Claude Code Usage by Trimming Prompt Bloat
highlights.summary: Headroom is a macOS menu bar app that silently compresses Claude Code's inputs by stripping boilerplate, trimming redundant context, and compressing documents before they hit the API. The result is roughly 2x more effective usage out of your existing Claude plan without changing how you interact with the tool.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial: Beginner to Advanced in 20 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube video promises a fast-paced walkthrough of Claude Code from zero to confident use in just 20 minutes — ideal for developers who want a quick but thorough on-ramp. It likely covers installation, basic commands, and some intermediate workflows, making it a solid first watch before diving into deeper topics.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: A focused 30-minute YouTube tutorial designed to take developers beyond the basics and into more productive workflows with Claude Code. If you've already tried Claude Code but feel like you're not getting the most out of it, this intermediate-level walkthrough is a great next step.

tips_of_the_day: If Claude Code starts behaving oddly after you've added hooks or MCP servers, launch it with `--safe-mode` to isolate the problem. This disables all customizations so you can confirm whether the issue is in your config or the core tool itself.

Before scaling a task to multiple subagents, test your prompt thoroughly with a single agent first. Bugs in your instructions multiply fast across a swarm — catching them early saves both time and API costs.

Use the `/cd` command to navigate between project directories within the same Claude Code session instead of restarting. This preserves your prompt cache, which means faster responses and lower token usage as you move between folders.
